app.title: Applications Hub
connDlg.title: Connect to SAP IBP
techReq.title: Technical requirements
techReq.conn.addrHelp: Replace {instancia} with the ID of your SAP IBP tenant.
techReq.conn.check4: ✓  Access enabled in SAP IBP (see SAP IBP Access tab)
techReq.user.type1: ✓  Must be a Communication User — a user dedicated to integration with external systems, separate from the regular business user
techReq.user.type2: ✓  Must have read permissions on planning data
techReq.user.type4: ✓  Must be linked to the configured integration scenario
techReq.user.howTitle: How to create it in SAP IBP
techReq.comm.intro: Each application needs a specific integration scenario enabled in SAP IBP. These scenarios control which data the application can access. Apps marked as offline do not require any setup in SAP IBP.
techReq.comm.scenarioDesc: Access to production data and IBP master data
techReq.comm.explorerHelp: Runs fully offline. You only need to upload the ZIP files exported from SAP CI-DS.
techReq.comm.setupTitle: How to configure access in SAP IBP
techReq.comm.setup1: 1.  Create a communication system in SAP IBP
techReq.comm.setup4: 4.  Select the scenario that matches your application
techReq.ent.title: OData entities consumed per application
techReq.ent.exOffline: Runs 100% offline — no SAP IBP connection required.
locked.title: Restricted module
locked.bom: The production hierarchy requires live data access. Please connect to your SAP IBP environment to continue.
locked.network: Network analysis evaluates multiple entities. Please connect to your SAP IBP environment to continue.
locked.visualizer: The dynamic visualizer needs to extract nodes and dependencies. Please connect to your SAP IBP environment to continue.
locked.pa: The production analyzer needs access to several SAP IBP entities. Please connect first.
banner.bom: Explore the BOM production structure of any material from SAP IBP. Navigate every level of the hierarchy, identify co-products and components, and see the associated production plants.
banner.network: Download and analyze the full logistics network for all materials. Generates an Excel report with resilience metrics, critical nodes, ghost nodes and overall supply network quality.
banner.visualizer: Interactively visualize the logistics network for a specific material. Confirm the mapping to load the material catalog, pick one and click "Load logistics network" to see its diagram.
banner.pa: Analyze consistency and quality of the production setup in SAP IBP. Detects components without procurement network, products not enabled at plants, components without Lead Time, and more. Download an Excel with all findings classified by severity.
banner.docs: Mapping Dataflow generator for SAP CI-DS integration tasks
banner.explorer: Visually explore CI-DS integrations. Upload ZIPs, navigate mappings/filters/lookups and detect chains of consecutive dataflows.
mdt.title: ENTITY MAPPING
mdt.autoDetect: Auto-detection assigned the most likely entities. Adjust manually if needed.
mdt.snHint: Select the master data entities to analyze the supply network. Auto-detection assigned the most likely ones.
mdt.vizHint: Select the entities. Auto-detection assigned the most likely ones. Confirm to load the product catalog.
guide.interp.title: Interpreting results
guide.interp.body: Check the Analyzers Glossary tab to understand the fields this analyzer returns and the analyses it performs.

viz.rutas.search: Search plant, end node or customer…
viz.filter.title: Network filters
docs.proTip: Pro tip: Connecting your SAP IBP system enriches the documentation with SAP IBP descriptions and data.
docs.zip.help: Export the project from CI-DS to get the compressed file, then drop it here. You can export and attach as many as you need.
docs.zip.dropHint: or click to browse  ·  Multiple files allowed
docs.zip.dropHintJobs: The same CI-DS exports you would use in ZIP mode
docs.atl.descZip: Upload the SAP Data Services ATL files to enrich the Excel with the process name and the group each dataflow belongs to. They are matched by GUID or dataflow name.
docs.jobs.desc: Connect your SAP IBP system to retrieve the configured Application Jobs. Requires Communication Arrangement SAP_COM_0326.
docs.atl.helpJobs: Export the CI-DS processes (tasks) in ATL format from SAP Data Services. The ATL file contains the execution structure: parallel groups and dataflow order.
docs.commGuide.title: 🔧 Setup: Communication Arrangement
docs.commGuide.intro: To use the Application Jobs feature, configure scenario SAP_COM_0326 in your SAP IBP system:
docs.commGuide.s1: Communication User: Create a technical user in Maintain Communication Users
docs.commGuide.s2: Communication System: Create a system in Communication Systems pointing to your IBP tenant
docs.commGuide.s3: Communication Arrangement: Create an arrangement with scenario SAP_COM_0326 (External Scheduler - Application Job Administration Integration)
docs.commGuide.s4: Assign the Communication User and System to the arrangement
docs.commGuide.s6: Use the same credentials in the GoSCM connection panel
docs.commGuide.outro: This Communication Arrangement exposes the BC_EXT_APPJOB_MANAGEMENT service, which lets you read Application Jobs and their steps.
docs.zipjobs.desc: Upload the same CI-DS ZIPs you would use in ZIP mode. The system parses them and automatically enriches them with SAP IBP Jobs and Steps using JobTemplateSequenceSet.
docs.zipjobs.analyzeBtn: ⚙️ Analyze ZIPs + Enrich with IBP Jobs

docs.stats.title: 📊 Result

xls.legend.detail: Validation detail. In OK status, lists those that passed.
xls.legend.role: Plant with Delivery = PSH + CustSrc | Plant = PSH only | DC with Direct Delivery = LocSrc + CustSrc | DC = LocSrc only | Delivery Point = CustSrc only | No active role = no network presence.
xls.legend.resilience: Single Path = some product reaches via a single route | Single Node Dependency = there is a single node whose failure cuts supply | Resilient = all products have alternative routes.
xls.legend.ltStatusT: OK = lead time > 0 | Zero = TLEADTIME = 0 | Missing = no value.
xls.legend.ltStatusC: OK = lead time > 0 | Zero = CLEADTIME = 0 | Missing = no value.
xls.obs.plantNotInLocSrc: Producing plant is not an origin in Location Source
xls.obs.noSupplyArc: No supply arc (not registered in Location Source)
xls.obs.semifNoPsi: Semi-finished without PSI consumption at producing plant nor configured transfer
xls.obs.coprodNoPrimary: Configured only as co-product (SOURCETYPE=C) — missing primary PSH
xls.obs.notConsumed: Not consumed as a component in any BOM
xls.obs.tleadAllZero: TLEADTIME = 0 in all Location Source arcs
xls.obs.resOrphan: Orphan resource: no use in production nor plant assigned
xls.obs.resNoPSR: No use in production (not in PSR)
xls.obs.resNoLocation: No plant assigned in Resource Location
xls.obs.resInUseWithLoc: In use in PSR and with plant assigned in Resource Location
xls.obs.bomComplete: BOM with PSI components | Lead time set | Enabled in LP | SOURCETYPE=P present | PSR resources assigned
xls.obs.locMasterIdle: Master-only location with no activity elsewhere
xls.obs.prdLocNoLP: PRDID+LOCID with no coverage in Location Product
xls.obs.multipleSourceIds: Multiple SOURCEIDs for same PRDID+LOCID — verify quotas
xls.obs.semifNoRecipe: Semi-finished without production recipe (PSH) at any plant
xls.obs.inputNoArc: Input without supply arc in Location Source
xls.obs.compNotInLP: Component not enabled in Location Product for this plant
xls.obs.altItemNotInSub: Replacement material without record in Item Sub
xls.obs.compExcludedType: Component of excluded type ({type}) — validated in context
xls.log.configureHeader: Configure at least the Production Source Header entity before analyzing
xls.log.pendingCorrections: There are pending corrections. Resolve them in the entity mapping step before running.
xls.log.excelDone: Excel downloaded! Analysis completed in {ms} ms

glosario.pdf.coverSubtitle: Reference guide for reading the exported analysis
main.bom.loadedSummary: ✓ {n} products in local cache. Pick one to view its BOM.
fm.panel.subtitle.issues.one: 1 compatibility issue was found with the schema of this SAP IBP system. Confirm how the application should handle it before proceeding.
fm.panel.subtitle.issues.many: {n} compatibility issues were found with the schema of this SAP IBP system. Confirm how the application should handle them before proceeding.
fm.panel.subtitle.active: The application is using the following saved corrections to adapt fields to your SAP IBP system.
fm.badge.null: does not exist in this system (omitted)
fm.warning.notFound: Auto-detection did not find any entity for this role. Indicate whether it exists in your IBP system or confirm it is not available.
fm.radio.notExists.entity: Does not exist in this system — skip this functionality
fm.warning.multiMissing: The app expects: {fields}. None were found in the schema. This entity may have a different role in your system.
fm.radio.notExists.field: Does not exist in my system — the app will work without this information
fm.warning.outOfSchema: This entity does not appear in the system's $metadata. The name may be incorrect. Check in the configuration panel.
fm.field.CLEADTIME: Customer delivery lead time in days. Detects arcs without lead time to customers.
fm.field.ISALTITEM: Indicates whether the PSI component is an alternative replacement material (X = substitute).
fm.field.PRDFR: Origin component product in the component substitution table.
fm.field.SPRDFR: Substitute product that replaces component PRDFR.
fm.field.TLEADTIME: Transfer lead time between locations in days.
fm.field.TINVALID: Invalidity flag for the transfer arc (X = inactive record).
fm.field.CINVALID: Invalidity flag for the customer delivery arc (X = inactive record).
fm.field.PINVALID: Invalidity flag for the production recipe (X = inactive record).
fm.field.LOCVALID: Invalidity flag for the master location (X = inactive location).
fm.field.CUSTVALID: Invalidity flag for the master customer (X = inactive customer).
fm.field.PRATIO: Production quota assigned to this source when there are multiple recipes for PRDID+LOCID.
fm.field.OUTPUTCOEFFICIENT: Finished product units generated per production run.
fm.field.SOURCETYPE: Source type (P = primary production, C = co-product / by-product).
fm.field.COMPONENTCOEFFICIENT: Component units consumed per unit of finished product.
fm.field.MATTYPEID: SAP material type. Determines planning categories.
